source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 4.4.3 ls command (pp. 44-45)

questions.q: List the contents of the current directory including hidden files.
questions.why: -a shows all files, including hidden ones (in Linux, files whose name begins with a dot).

questions.q: In Linux, which files are 'hidden' by default?
options: Files whose name begins with a dot (.)
Files whose name begins with an underscore
questions.why: Hidden files are simply those beginning with '.'; -a (or -A) reveals them.

questions.q: List a directory in long format showing full details for each entry.
questions.why: -l uses a long listing format, one line of detailed information per file or directory.

questions.q: Which ls option displays the inode number of each entry?
questions.why: -i prints inode numbers; it is the value in the first column of 'ls -lia' output.

questions.q: Long-list a directory with file sizes in human-readable units (KB, MB, GB).
questions.why: -h means Human Readable and must be combined with -l to show sizes like 1.2M instead of raw bytes.

questions.q: Show the details of the directory /etc ITSELF (its own permissions), not the files inside it.
questions.why: By default ls lists a directory's contents; -d makes it report the directory itself instead.

questions.q: Which ls option lists the contents of subdirectories recursively?
questions.why: -R recurses into subdirectories; lowercase -r instead reverses the sort order.

questions.q: With 'ls -F', which suffix marks a directory and which marks an executable?
options: A directory gets '/', an executable gets '*'
A directory gets '*', an executable gets '/'
A directory gets '@', an executable gets '/'
questions.why: -F appends a type indicator: '/' for a directory, '*' for an executable, '@' for a symbolic link, nothing for a plain text file.

questions.q: In the output line '78489 drwx------ 4 rockstar rockstar 4096 25 oct. 08:10 rockstar', what is the first field, 78489?
options: The user ID of the owner
questions.why: The leading number is the inode; 'drwx------' is the type+rights, '4' the link/subdirectory count, then owner, group, size, date and name.
